candidates.evidence_quote: primarily due to additional revenue related to new restaurant openings and an increase in comparable restaurant sales
We plan to open as many as 22 new restaurants in fiscal 2024
partially offset by decreased customer traffic of 0.2%
We implemented effective menu price increases of approximately 2.5% and 2.0% in the first quarter of fiscal 2024 and the third quarter of fiscal 2023, respectively.
primarily driven by an increase in average check of 1.6% (based on an increase of 4.5% in menu pricing and 2.9% negative impact from mix)
Average check variations are driven by menu price increases and/or changes in menu mix.
North Italia comparable sales increased approximately 2% from the second quarter of fiscal 2023.
Sales through the off-premise channel comprised approximately 21% of our restaurant sales during the second quarter of fiscal 2024

food and beverage costs were 22.3% and 23.2% in the second quarters of fiscal 2024 and 2023, respectively, primarily due to menu price increases in excess of inflation across most categories (0.4%) and a shift in sales mix (0.4%)
We anticipate commodity inflation to be in the low-single digit range and expect labor inflation to be in the mid-single digit range.
This decrease is primarily driven by menu price increases in excess of wage rate inflation and improved productivity (0.4%)
labor expenses, which include restaurant-level labor costs and bakery production labor, including associated fringe benefits, were 35.1% and 35.3% in the second quarters of fiscal 2024 and 2023
menu price increases in excess of wage rate inflation and improved productivity (0.4%)
primarily driven by increased occupancy and building related costs (0.4%)
partially offset by increased restaurant-level incentive compensation expense (0.1%)
This variance was primarily due to lower legal fees (0.2%).
G&A expenses were 6.0% and 6.4% in the second quarter of fiscal 2024 and 2023, respectively
Preopening costs were $7.0 million and $6.0 million in the second quarters of fiscal 2024 and 2023, respectively.
we recorded impairment of assets and lease terminations expense of $1.9 million primarily related to impairment of assets for one The Cheesecake Factory location
lease termination costs for one The Cheesecake Factory and one Flower Child location
Our effective income tax rate was 6.9% and 5.8% for the second quarters of fiscal 2024 and 2023, respectively.
The increase was primarily due to a lower proportion of employment credits in relation to income before income taxes (3.0%)
we anticipate total revenue for fiscal 2024 to be approximately $3.56 billion to $3.60 billion
we expect fiscal 2024 net income margin of approximately 4.3% to 4.4% based on the estimated revenue range provided
We anticipate approximately $180 to $200 million in cash capital expenditures to support this level of unit development
returning capital to shareholders through our dividend and share repurchase programs, the latter of which offsets dilution from our equity compensation program
Future decisions to pay or to increase or decrease dividends or to repurchase shares are at the discretion of the Board
We have also encountered delays in opening new restaurants primarily due to delays in permitting and landlord readiness, as well as supply chain challenges.
Restaurant opening dates may be impacted by supply chain challenges and permit approval delays.
Cash flows from operations decreased by $7.1 million from the first six months of fiscal 2023 primarily due to an increase in inventory levels
increases in minimum wages and benefit costs
foreign exchange rates, tariffs and cross border taxation
foreign exchange rates, tariffs and cross border taxation
economic, public health and political conditions that impact consumer confidence and spending

notes: Chunk contains 1 event: 10-Q MD&A part 1/2 (accession 0001410578-24-001151, 2024-08-05). No fiscal_kpis in this chunk.

Cheesecake Factory brand comps not coined as a brand driver because the brand name equals the company legal name (banned); covered by same_store_sales. North Italia is a non-company brand, so its comp metric is its own driver.
